claim: WHO estimates 236000 drowning deaths globally in 2019, children under five at highest risk, over 90 percent in LMICs, with South Asia having the highest absolute numbers.
method: Reviewed WHO drowning report, mortality data, and prevention recommendations.
limitations: Mortality data depends on verbal autopsy in areas with incomplete vital registration.
Drowning deaths are undercounted in many LMICs.

claim: A 2019 study found that community creche programs in Bangladesh reduced child drowning by over 80 percent through supervised childcare during peak-risk hours, demonstrating prevention feasibility.

source.title: Effectiveness of a community-based child drowning prevention programme in rural Bangladesh

method: Reviewed intervention design, drowning-mortality comparison, and effectiveness estimates.
limitations: Study conducted in specific rural Bangladesh context.
Creche effectiveness depends on sustained community engagement.
